skill.name: resilience-analysis
skill.description: Assess error handling, isolation boundaries, recovery mechanisms, and production readiness in agent frameworks.
skill.summary: Map agent failure paths and identify resilience gaps before production release.
skill.author: Dowwie

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found high-risk code execution strings and many shell backtick indicators. Manual review shows these are markdown documentation examples for resilience analysis, not executable skill code. No evidence found of prompt injection, malicious intent, network use, or credential exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Documented Dynamic Execution Examples
low_findings.description: The exec, eval, and shell subprocess patterns are shown inside fenced Python examples under the No Sandboxing section. They demonstrate unsafe patterns for reviewers to detect and are not invoked by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious calls appear only in markdown code fences and are labeled as no sandboxing examples. No runnable script or command wrapper calls them.

low_findings.title: Documented Subprocess Isolation Example
low_findings.description: The subprocess.run call is part of a process isolation example with timeout and privilege dropping. It is illustrative documentation, not executable marketplace code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code is fenced markdown inside a sandboxing guidance section. There is no supporting file that executes the example.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified As Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The Ruby shell backtick findings correspond to markdown code fences and inline formatting. They do not represent shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each cited location is a markdown fence or documentation template. The skill contains only SKILL.md and no executable Ruby or shell file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detector False Positive
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings point to descriptive text and a helper name in pseudocode. No cryptographic algorithm or crypto API call is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no DES, MD5, SHA1, or other weak cryptographic implementation. The matches are lexical noise in prose and pseudocode.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unsafe Execution Patterns Shown As Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents exec, eval, and shell subprocess usage as unsafe patterns for resilience review. These examples should remain clearly framed as anti-patterns.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples are in a section titled No Sandboxing and are used to explain risks. They are not connected to executable skill behavior.

content.value_statement: Agent frameworks can fail through hidden propagation paths, weak isolation, and missing recovery controls. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured resilience analysis.
actual_capabilities: Maps how tool, parser, API, timeout, and state errors propagate.
Compares sandboxing approaches for code execution and resource isolation.
Catalogs retry, fallback, and circuit breaker mechanisms.
Identifies failures that terminate agents instead of being contained.
Produces a structured production readiness assessment.
limitations: Requires access to relevant framework code or architecture notes.
Does not run tests or prove runtime behavior by itself.
Does not replace formal threat modeling or penetration testing.
Uses examples that must be adapted to each framework.

use_cases.title: Review agent framework release readiness
use_cases.description: Assess whether failures are contained before shipping an agent framework to production.

use_cases.title: Audit sandboxing for tool execution
use_cases.description: Check whether code execution, files, network access, and resource limits have clear isolation boundaries.

use_cases.title: Improve recovery behavior after failures
use_cases.description: Find missing retries, fallbacks, and circuit breakers that affect reliability.

prompt_templates.title: Map basic error flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one agent workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use resilience-analysis to map how tool errors, parser errors, and API errors move through this agent workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Assess sandboxing boundaries
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of code execution controls.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use resilience-analysis to evaluate sandboxing for tool execution, file access, network access, and resource limits.

prompt_templates.title: Catalog recovery mechanisms
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced reliability review across several components.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use resilience-analysis to identify retries, fallbacks, circuit breakers, and gaps across the agent framework.

prompt_templates.title: Produce readiness assessment
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced release review for production deployment.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use resilience-analysis to create a production readiness assessment with critical gaps and recommended fixes.

output_examples.input: Analyze how an agent handles rate limits, parser failures, and tool timeouts.
output_examples.output: The report identifies each failure source, expected handling, propagation behavior, and whether the failure blocks production readiness.

output_examples.input: Review sandboxing for a framework that executes generated Python.
output_examples.output: The assessment compares current isolation with subprocess, container, and resource limit options, then flags missing controls.

output_examples.input: Check whether fallback model behavior is reliable.
output_examples.output: The output explains when fallback triggers, which errors bypass it, and where circuit breaker protection is missing.

best_practices: Start with a codebase map so execution paths are clear.
Separate documented design intent from actual runtime behavior.
Record evidence for each resilience claim with file locations.
anti_patterns: Assuming retries exist because the framework mentions resilience.
Treating subprocess isolation as sufficient without resource limits.
Ignoring parser and state failures during production review.

faq.question: What does this skill analyze?
faq.answer: It analyzes error propagation, sandboxing, recovery mechanisms, and production readiness in agent frameworks.

faq.question: Does it execute code?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides analysis guidance and examples, but it does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: Can it find security issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It can highlight weak isolation, unsafe execution paths, and failures that expose sensitive operations.

faq.question: What input works best?
faq.answer: Provide framework code, architecture notes, error handling modules, and tool execution paths.

faq.question: Is it only for Python frameworks?
faq.answer: No. The examples use Python, but the review method applies to other agent frameworks.

faq.question: Does it prove production readiness?
faq.answer: No. It supports review, but runtime tests and operational validation are still required.
